title: CRS 001 — Module 3 Flask App Flow
subtitle: Watsonx auth, per-model tokens, JSON parser, config → model → app

nodes.label: SN Auth Pattern
nodes.definition: Credentials(url only) + project_id=skills-network — no api_key in code on Cloud IDE.
nodes.whyItMatters: Explicit api_key caused empty generated_text in bite 1 debug.
nodes.safeSentence: Match PDF auth before debugging prompt tokens.
nodes.commonTrap: Passing WATSONX_APIKEY into Credentials on Skills Network.
nodes.interviewAnswer: I use environment-injected bearer auth on SN; I avoid conflicting api_key in code.

nodes.label: Per-Model Tokens
nodes.definition: Granite <|system|>; Llama header tokens; Mistral [INST] — same question, different dialects.
nodes.whyItMatters: Plain prompts on Llama/Granite can return garbage or empty output.
nodes.safeSentence: Each foundation model has a preferred prompt shape.

nodes.label: ChatWatsonx Pipe
nodes.whyItMatters: Bridge from raw ModelInference to reusable chains.

nodes.label: AIResponse JSON
nodes.definition: Pydantic AIResponse + JsonOutputParser → dict with summary, sentiment, response.
nodes.whyItMatters: Downstream workflow systems need validated fields, not prose only.
nodes.safeSentence: Parser enforces shape; Flask jsonifies the dict.

nodes.label: config.py
nodes.definition: Model IDs, GenParams, credentials constants — settings only.
nodes.whyItMatters: Single place to swap Maverick for PDF Llama ID on SN.

nodes.label: model.py utility
nodes.whyItMatters: HTTP layer stays thin; all AI logic lives here.
nodes.safeSentence: llm_test imports model; model imports config.
nodes.commonTrap: Putting LangChain chains directly in Flask routes with no model.py.

nodes.label: POST /generate
nodes.definition: Flask reads message + model name, calls handler, adds duration, jsonify.
nodes.whyItMatters: Production-shaped API boundary — same pattern as FastAPI later.

note.summary: curl POST /generate with granite|llama|mistral — proved on SN VM.
